key_insight: The paper argues that legal sentence calculation should be split into neural semantic extraction and symbolic/statutory verification to avoid LLM hallucinations in Korean criminal law workflows.
engineering_relevance: This is a practical neuro-symbolic design for high-stakes decision support, where deterministic solvers can enforce statutory arithmetic while humans retain final oversight.
method: LLM extracts structured facts from unstructured legal text.
SMT solver computes sentencing/fine outcomes from formalized 2026 traffic-offense guidelines.
Human-in-the-loop review checks the generated indictment or sentencing draft.
evidence_signal: The abstract claims the framework formalizes the 2026 Sentencing Guidelines for Traffic Offenses and supports deterministic summary indictment drafting, but it does not report quantitative evaluation metrics.
watch_item: The approach depends on accurate legal text extraction and up-to-date formal rules, so coverage gaps or statute changes could still lead to errors.
paper_title: Neuro-Symbolic AI for Korean Criminal Law: Sentencing Prediction and Document Drafting
